approach: audit the corpus against the current governances before repairing anything, and report the findings at the spec gate
repair only confirmed contract gaps; restructure nothing that already conforms and invent no new spec content
treat provenance records as read-only: audit ledger, combat-log, and plan-brief format drift and report it, never retrofit a past record to a newer contract
surface the pending unratified strategy recommendations for the Council rather than ratifying or deleting them

summary: both behavioral nodes exceed the suite granularity threshold; route breadth-vs-depth now that the scenario maps exist to show which rows are real permutation coverage
evidence: the corpus structure audit reports both nodes oversized against the threshold, with a breadth hint from their section-cluster counts
the maps this change added are the input the routing judgment was previously missing

summary: a handful of suite scenarios assert an internal call rather than an observable trace, use a disjunctive precondition, or use an evaluative assertion
evidence: surfaced by the suite-format audit against the current contract
each one edits a frozen suite, so they need their own scoped change rather than folding into a structural repair

summary: the scenario-map lint recognizes a row only when the scenario cell is backtick-wrapped, which the governance prose never states; an unwrapped map reads as complete and binds nothing
evidence: hit inside this change: one map landed textually one-to-one with its suite and every row was invisible to the check
the same silent-skip shape the missing maps themselves had, so the guard against it is itself guardless

summary: most plan briefs carry only their todo list, missing the change-request, source, project and status keys, so paused missions cannot be discovered or cleared for dispatch
evidence: the provenance audit found the full key set on a minority of briefs
two different terminal words are in use for the same finished state
